task_completion.description: Revenue, Sessions, and Bounce Rate columns are formatted as numeric values
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Power Query Editor or Data view, the 'Revenue', 'Sessions', and 'Bounce Rate' columns should display numeric data type icons (like '1.2' or '123') in their headers, and the data cells should not contain commas or '%' symbols.

task_completion.description: DAX measure named Revenue_Per_Session is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Fields/Data pane on the right shows a measure named 'Revenue_Per_Session' (indicated by a calculator icon). The formula bar may also show the DAX formula dividing Total Revenue by Total Sessions.

task_completion.description: Line Chart showing Revenue by Month of the year with Year as legend
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line chart visual is present on the canvas. The X-axis displays months, the Y-axis displays revenue values, and there are distinct lines for different years (2019 and 2020) indicated by a legend.

task_completion.description: Table showing Source / Medium sorted by Revenue descending
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table visual is present containing 'Source / Medium' (e.g., A, B, C) and 'Revenue' columns. The table must be sorted so the highest revenue values appear at the top.
A card visual (single value display) is present on the canvas, explicitly labeled 'Revenue_Per_Session' beneath the number.
A slicer visual is present on the canvas containing the 'Year' field (showing options for 2019 and 2020).
The Power BI Desktop window title bar reads 'Channel_Performance - Power BI Desktop', or a file explorer window shows 'Channel_Performance.pbix' saved on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to build the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Power BI Desktop application interface with menus, ribbons, and canvas being actively used, rather than a script or terminal window.

integrity.description: Visuals reflect the actual dataset provided
integrity.visual_evidence: The visuals display expected data points: years 2019/2020 in the slicer/legend, and anonymized letters (A, B, C, etc.) in the Source / Medium table, rather than dummy text.

integrity.description: The card visual uses the actual DAX measure, not a hardcoded text box
integrity.visual_evidence: The card visual should be a genuine Power BI card visualization bound to the 'Revenue_Per_Session' field, not a static text box inserted to look like a card.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 250 rows spanning years 2019 and 2020. 'Revenue' and 'Sessions' initially contain commas, and 'Bounce Rate' contains '%' symbols; these must be cleaned to numeric types in Power Query or Data view. 'Source / Medium' values are anonymized letters (A, B, C, etc.). The required DAX measure is 'Revenue_Per_Session'. The final file must be saved as 'Channel_Performance.pbix'.
